meta.title: The Tenebrous Reliquary
meta.author: Anders Arpi, Ben H., Dom Cohen, Ripley C., Johan Nohr, Karl Druid, Leander E., Paul Wilde, Flora v/d B. (MBC: Tenebrous Reliquary)
meta.description: Thirty-six accursed objects. A d66 cursed-item supplement, not a scenario. The GM places the reliquary; the players regret it.
meta.license: Based on official Mörk Borg content. Independent production by Trevor Burkholder, published under the MÖRK BORG Third Party License. MÖRK BORG is © Ockult Örtmästare Games and Stockholm Kartell.

nodes.title: The Reliquary, Found
nodes.atmosphere: Velvet that should have rotted. Brass clasps that open without sound. Inside, thirty-six small wrongnesses arranged in rows, like teeth.
nodes.read_aloud: You open the container and inside, on velvet that should have rotted by now, are thirty-six objects. Each is wrong in its own way. A flail black with old blood. A hook still wet. A flower the color of a stopped vein. A vial of tears that you are certain are not water. You feel, the moment your eyes pass over them, that each has been waiting for a hand exactly like yours.

contents.description: The Tenebrous Reliquary is not a place — it is a vessel. A vault behind a heretic's altar. A lacquered chest in a sorcerer's tower. A muddy satchel on a corpse the crows would not touch. The container is GM-placed; the contents are not. Inside lie thirty-six relics, weapons, and curse-objects. The PCs may take any, all, or none. Each will demand its price in time.
items: The reliquary itself — a container (chest, vault, satchel, altar drawer — GM's choice). Empty once looted; the velvet within blackens and rots in d6 days, as if it had been waiting for someone to take the items away before allowing itself to die.
Thirty-six numbered objects. See the d66 table at THE RELIQUARY for full descriptions, or scroll the items list there for one-line summaries.
secrets: Whoever assembled the reliquary did so deliberately — the objects do not occur together in nature. Someone hunted these down. Someone who is probably dead. Probably.

Some of the items resonate with each other when touched: the Plasmatic Idol weeps when the Cup of Peace is near; the Black Candles flicker harder in the presence of the Chaos Blade. The GM may invent further resonances at the table.

rules.title: Rolling d66
rules.text: Roll two d6 (or one d6 twice). The first die is the tens digit, the second is the ones. Read as a two-digit number from 11 to 66. There is no 0, so the table contains exactly 36 results: 11–16, 21–26, 31–36, 41–46, 51–56, 61–66.

rules.title: Using the Reliquary
rules.text: The reliquary is not a dungeon. It is a delivery system. Drop it into any adventure where the party needs a cursed gift — at the bottom of a tomb, in a wizard's locked room, in the pack of an executed cultist. Let the players choose freely, or roll for them. Items may be sold (if a buyer is fool enough), traded, gifted to enemies, or buried. None of these acts remove them from the world for long.

nodes.gm_notes: This node exists as setup. The real reference is THE RELIQUARY. Read the read-aloud once the PCs open the container, then move directly to the item table. If the PCs are cautious — wisely — let them examine items one at a time before touching; many of the relics' downsides are visible (a hook wet with someone else's blood; a tongue still moving; a cauldron warm without fire) and a Presence test of the GM's choice may surface a single hint. The reliquary itself has no monetary value once looted. The container rots in d6 days regardless of what is done with it.

exits.label: Examine the contents — open the d66 catalog

nodes.title: The Reliquary — d66 Items of Doom
nodes.atmosphere: Thirty-six wrongnesses in a row. The velvet does not breathe. The objects, occasionally, do.
nodes.read_aloud: These are the thirty-six. Choose, or do not. Roll, or do not. The reliquary does not mind either way. It has time.

contents.description: The full d66 cursed-item table. Each item is named, numbered, and mechanically described. Use the Items list below for at-a-glance summaries, and the Rules section for the full row-by-row mechanics. The GM should let players roll d66 themselves where appropriate — the surprise is part of the curse.
items: 11 — Mercy's Bane: spiked flail d8, +d6 damage to surrendering enemies. The flail knows the difference between a raised blade and an open palm.
12 — Opium Hook: hook in artery d4 dmg, 2× temp HP for 2 hours; addiction risk Toughness DR10, rising each failure. The hole never closes.
13 — Plasmatic Idol: spill blood, roll d6 — odd, poisoned (DR12 Toughness or −10 HP); even, +2 DR to Agility/Strength until next rest.
14 — Ripper's Blade: no damage. Removes d4−1 Armor per hit. Wants the shell, not the flesh.
15 — Roses of Winter: eat a petal — numb to all pain, cannot be killed or broken for a few minutes. Damage still accrues.
16 — Spine of God: bearer attracts the poor with unknown intent. If broken: clouds part, sun shines for the first time in a century.
21 — Snort Dagger: dripping reeking blade. Killing a goblin breaks it forever but frees the cursed creature within.
22 — Stone Magnet: paired stones, round glows orange near danger. The stones can lie.
23 — Tentacles of Zen: rust lamp, shadow tendrils. Presence DR14 — pass: −1 Presence; fail: +1 Presence. The lamp does not care.
24 — Tyrant's Tongue: in a skull, the skull stands and screams its last words. Forever.
25 — Veil of Blood: placed on enemy's head — tightens, d6 damage per round. Victims rise next round as a Blood-drenched Skeleton.
26 — Voodoo Fire: lamp of eternal green flame. Smashed: d4 fire dmg, spreads with oil. Extinguished only by the tears of SHE.
31 — Volt Thrower: javelin d6. Crit: +2d10 lightning, ignores armor. Fumble: lightning strikes wielder before release.
32 — War Starter: crimson round shield. Onlookers Presence DR14 or provoked to attack its wielder.
33 — Zodiac Lung: absorbed into chest. Breathe filthy water. After 1h underwater, expel sludge for d4 damage to nearest target.
34 — Cup of Peace: drink — Toughness DR16 or die of acute poisoning. There is no second sip.
35 — Dust of Paradise: inhale — Presence DR14 or lost in joyful visions for d6 minutes or until damaged.
36 — Ebony Tears: black vial. Contact: Presence DR14 or fail next morale check; outside combat, despair for 3d10 minutes.
41 — Eye of Horus: forehead. See your deaths in trap-checking timelines. Overland travel mired in walking through corpses.
42 — Finger Paintings of the Insane: touching paint — Toughness DR12 or paint heresy for a minute. Painted scrolls: −d4 to cast.
43 — Flower of Disease: spreading thorny weed, swallows a village in two days. Beaten by boiled oil. Fire does nothing.
44 — Foehammer: warhammer 2d6. Wielder forms a new grudge daily, lasting until they bash that head in. Neglect = infection.
45 — Cauldron of Lies: drinks brewed and shared let all imbibers detect lies for a month.
46 — Cursed Tongue of the Naga: in a skull — the skull stands and screams bloody filth while running on its tongue-tips.
51 — Chaos Blade: zweihänder 2d8. On maximum damage, the Power Death is cast.
52 — Chains of Death: frigid black manacles. If you die wearing them, they ensnare your killer and drag them to another world.
53 — Claw of the Sloth: dagger d4. On a 1, target's DR −2 next round; reaching DR 0 freezes them in place, permanently.
54 — Blood of the Serpents: acidic poison. Infected's blood sprays on attackers — Agility DR12 or lose Armor tier and become infected.
55 — Crown of Burning Stars: adds Omens equal to Miseries transpired to each PC's pool. Miseries now occur on 1 & 2.
56 — Book of Oblivion: sentient tome. Presence DR12 to give it a thought/memory permanently. Fail: it takes extra.
61 — Antlers of Lightning: crackling mantle. Reading any scroll, flip a coin — Heads, scroll becomes Nine Violet Signs Unknot the Storm. Death strips flesh to bone.
62 — Eyes and Teeth: replace d10 teeth, d2 eyes. Toughness DR14 or rejection-infection. Roll d4 each for effects (see Rules).
63 — Black Candles: lit, jet-black flame flickers toward the greatest threat within a mile.
64 — Robe of Bones: once daily, stare and fold — target Toughness DR13 or a random limb's bone snaps soundlessly.
65 — Ash of the Mind: sigil on palm, touch a sentient — read their thoughts. Their Presence DR12 or their thoughts become yours.
66 — Bowels of a Baby Killer: drape entrails, gain monstrous scent. Evil creatures DR14 to find you. Fail: you pass unnoticed.
secrets: Several items hint at things outside the bestiary: the warm sunbeam of Spine of God (the sky has been black for a century), the tears of SHE (a wept divinity, unnamed), the 'another world' of Chains of Death (somewhere the killers go), and the Calendar-accelerating Crown of Burning Stars (apocalypse on a schedule). The GM is invited to make these consequences real.

The d66 numbering implies room for entries in the gaps — each row has six slots numbered 1–6, but the table only ever uses x1–x6. There is no x0 and no x7+. The shape of the table itself is an invitation to write more.
If all 36 items leave the reliquary, the velvet inside begins to bleed. Slowly. As if it misses them.

rules.title: Row 11–16 (roll d6 for the ones digit after a 1)
rules.text: 11 MERCY'S BANE — Spiked Flail (d8). Additional d6 damage to surrendering enemies. The flail knows the difference between a raised blade and an open palm; it prefers the palm. 12 OPIUM HOOK — Metal hook driven into an artery (d4 dmg). Grants 2× the user's HP as temp HP for 2 hours, opiate-like. Toughness DR10 to resist dependency; every sober day deals infection damage and DR rises with each failed attempt. Eventually addicted; temp-HP benefit lost. The hole never closes. 13 PLASMATIC IDOL — Spill blood on it while resting and roll d6: odd, the blood becomes red poison (Toughness DR12 or −10 HP); even, until next rest Agility and Strength tests are +2 DR. 14 RIPPER'S BLADE — Deals no damage but removes d4−1 Armor per hit. It does not wound flesh. It only wants the shell. 15 ROSES OF WINTER — A near-extinct blood-red thorny flower (not a true rose). A single eaten petal numbs the consumer to all pain for a few minutes; damage is received normally but the eater cannot be killed or broken during the effect. 16 SPINE OF GOD — A shriveled length of vertebrae, warm to the touch. The bearer attracts the poor, intentions unknown even to themselves. If broken: the clouds part — warm sunbeams pierce the heavens for the first time in a century.

rules.title: Row 21–26
rules.text: 21 SNORT DAGGER — Disgusting dripping blade reeking of excrement and rot. Killing a goblin permanently breaks the blade but undoes the curse, freeing the creature trapped within. 22 STONE MAGNET — A pair of magnetic stones that crackle and fizz when rubbed. When the square stone is near danger, the round stone glows dull orange. The stones can lie. 23 TENTACLES OF ZEN — Shadow tendrils creep from a rusty lamp when rubbed. Presence DR14 — on success the brain struggles and breaks (−1 Presence); otherwise the tentacles take root without resistance (+1 Presence). The lamp does not care which way it goes. 24 TYRANT'S TONGUE — Placed in a skull's mouth, the skull stands upright and screams its last words before dying. Over and over and over again. 25 VEIL OF BLOOD — Place this crimson shroud upon an enemy's head — it tightens, chokes, strangles, crushes the skull, drowning the torso in blood (d6 damage per round). Victims who succumb arise the next round as a Blood-drenched Skeleton, attacking at random. 26 VOODOO FIRE — Eternally burning green flame inside an oil lamp. Smashed, the flame spreads with the scattered oil — d4 fire damage on impact. Can only be extinguished by the tears of SHE.

rules.title: Row 31–36
rules.text: 31 VOLT THROWER — Ancient javelin of unidentifiable charred and pitted metal (d6 damage). On a critical hit a lightning bolt strikes the javelin for an additional 2d10 damage, not reduced by armor. On a fumble the lightning strikes the wielder before the javelin is released. Use indoors is cautioned. 32 WAR STARTER — Heavy metal round shield emblazoned with a bright crimson pattern. Anyone looking at it tests Presence DR14 or is provoked to attack its wielder. 33 ZODIAC LUNG — Pressing this blackened organ to the chest absorbs it, leaving fetid scars and pustules. The user may breathe underwater, provided the water is unclean. After an hour of submersion, the user may expunge the filth as writhing sludge dealing d4 damage to the closest target. 34 CUP OF PEACE — A beautiful ceremonial gold-and-silver cup. Drinking from it triggers a Toughness DR16 or the drinker dies of acute poisoning. There is no second sip. 35 DUST OF PARADISE — Fine pale-white ashes in a void-black pouch. Anyone inhaling rolls Presence DR14 or is lost in visions of a long-lost pleasant world of joy and mirth, lasting d6 minutes or until damaged. The transfixed cannot act, perceive, or respond in any way to the real world. 36 EBONY TEARS — A vial of black liquid. On contact a living creature tests Presence DR14 or is overcome with grief — immediately failing a morale check in combat. Outside combat the affected acts as if indifferent, unable to attack as it struggles with despair for 3d10 minutes.

rules.title: Row 41–46
rules.text: 41 EYE OF HORUS — Placed upon the forehead, the eye grants vision into the abyss at a price. When checking for traps, you see your grisly death from a less-careful timeline and may better find a workaround. Overland travel is mired with difficulty as you wade through the corpses. 42 FINGER PAINTINGS OF THE INSANE — Slick oily paints, forever wet upon an old wooden palette. Those touching any colour test Toughness DR12 or lose themselves for a minute painting the nearest surface with heretical imagery. Marking scroll text with the paint imbues it with those lost to the pigments' souls — −d4 from the Presence test to cast. 43 FLOWER OF DISEASE — Seeds for a rapidly spreading thorny weed with a poisonous aroma. Can swallow a small village in two days if not fought. Weak against boiled oil. Fire does nothing. 44 FOEHAMMER — Cursed warhammer (2d6 damage) whose wielder develops a new grudge with a random person daily, lasting until Foehammer caves their head in. Neglecting a grudge for too long counts as an infection. 45 CAULDRON OF LIES — Beverages cooked in this cauldron and shared allow all imbibers to recognize when another is lying for a month. Used in cults and secret societies to single out traitors. 46 CURSED TONGUE OF THE NAGA — Putting this long forked tongue into a skull pushes the skull upright, screaming bloody filth while running about on each tongue-tip.

rules.title: Row 51–56
rules.text: 51 CHAOS BLADE — Zweihänder dealing 2d8 damage. On maximum damage the Power Death is cast. 52 CHAINS OF DEATH — A set of frigid black manacles. If you die wearing them, they unlatch, ensnare your killer, and drag them to another world before vanishing. 53 CLAW OF THE SLOTH — Dagger (d4 damage). On a 1, attack and defense DR against that target are −2 next round. If DR reaches 0 the target is frozen in place, permanently. 54 BLOOD OF THE SERPENTS — Green-red liquid, highly acidic and poisonous. Turns blood neon-green and spreads through bodily liquids. When the infected is attacked, the attacker tests Agility DR12 to dodge a spray of blood — reduces Armor one tier and transmits the condition. 55 CROWN OF BURNING STARS — Adds Omens equal to the number of Miseries (Calendar of Nechrubel) that have transpired to each character's pool. Miseries now occur on 1 & 2 instead of 1 only. The apocalypse comes faster while you wear it. 56 BOOK OF OBLIVION — Sentient black leather tome with blank pages. With Presence DR12, a creature permanently transfers knowledge — a thought or a memory — to the book, forgetting the thing transferred. On failure the book takes something extra. You can learn anything the book previously absorbed in exchange for something you don't want to give it.

rules.title: Row 61–66
rules.text: 61 ANTLERS OF LIGHTNING — A crackling mantle. When reading any scroll, flip a coin. On Heads, the scroll's effects are replaced by Nine Violet Signs Unknot the Storm. Upon the wearer's death, lightning rends the flesh from bone, leaving only the mantle. 62 EYES AND TEETH — Replace d10 missing teeth and d2 eyes. Toughness DR14 or rejection causes infection. EYES (d4): 1 Wandering (DR +2 to ranged attacks); 2 Morbid Visions (every rest, share your nightmare with the group); 3 Let there be light! (no ill effects); 4 SIGHT BEYOND SIGHT! (Presence DR10 for eyes to twitch near danger — but it's not YOUR eye, it can lie). TEETH (d4): 1 Toothache (DR +2 to Powers' Presence roll); 2 Functional but hideous; 3 You got teeth! (no ill effects); 4 FANGS! (bite d6; on 1–2 an enemy gets a free attack). 63 BLACK CANDLES — When lit, the jet-black flame flickers toward the greatest threat within a mile (or kilometer). 64 ROBE OF BONES — This lumpy cape rattles in the wind. Once daily, the wearer may stare blankly at a humanoid creature as the cape guides their hands. With a fold of the robe, the target tests Toughness DR13 or a random limb's bone snaps soundlessly as the robe crackles. 65 ASH OF THE MIND — Smear a sigil onto one's palm and touch another sentient being to read their thoughts. Their Presence DR12 test, or their thoughts become yours. 66 BOWELS OF A BABY KILLER — Take the bowl and drape the entrails over you, gaining a monster's scent. Evil creatures must make a DR14 check when attempting to discern your location. Should they fail, you pass by unnoticed.

nodes.gm_notes: This is a reference screen, not a scene. Use it the way you would use a printed table — let players roll openly, or assign items deliberately as cursed gifts, dungeon loot, or villain inventories. CROSS-REFERENCES: Veil of Blood (25) victims become Blood-drenched Skeletons (statted in the wiki); Voodoo Fire (26) extinguishes only to SHE's tears; Chaos Blade (51) max damage casts the Power Death; Crown of Burning Stars (55) accelerates the Calendar of Nechrubel (Miseries fire on 1 & 2). The d66 numbering implies room for entries in the gaps — 11–16, 21–26, etc. are exactly six per row — homebrew expansion is invited by the table's own shape. If you add a 17 or a 27, the reliquary does not object. It has always been the right size for whatever you put in it.

exits.label: Close the reliquary — return to the moment of finding it
